fields.label: Authentication Provider
fields.description: Specify which AWS credentials chain to use.

fields.label: Credentials Profile Name
fields.description: Credentials profile name, as specified in ~/.aws/credentials, leave blank for default.

fields.label: Assume Role ARN
fields.description: Optional. Specifying the ARN of a role will ensure that the selected authentication provider is used to assume the role rather than the credentials directly.

validations.message: When set, must be a valid IAM role ARN, e.g. arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole

fields.label: External ID
fields.description: If you are assuming a role in another account, that has been created with an external ID, specify the external ID here.

fields.label: Endpoint
fields.description: Optionally, specify a custom endpoint for the service

fields.label: Default Region
fields.description: Specify the region, such as for US West (Oregon) use ` us-west-2 ` as the region.

relationships.description: AWS access key pair — supplied together when authType is 'keys'
STS assume-role parameters — external ID is only meaningful when assumeRoleArn is set
AWS Secrets Manager selection — the editor writes both fields from a single Select whose value is the secret ARN and whose label is the secret name
Redshift provisioning model — useServerless=false requires clusterIdentifier (Provisioned); useServerless=true requires workgroupName (Serverless)

Redshift credential mode — useManagedSecret=false requires dbUser (temporary IAM creds via GetClusterCredentials/GetCredentials); useManagedSecret=true requires managedSecret.arn (Secrets Manager stores both dbUser and password)

instructions.msg: Six authentication values are stored in jsonData.authType. The five editor-selectable ones are: 'default' (AWS SDK Default — the default credential chain), 'keys' (Access & secret key — puts accessKey/secretKey in secureJsonData), 'credentials' (Credentials file — optionally provide jsonData.profile), 'ec2_iam_role' (Workspace IAM Role — no extra credentials needed), and 'grafana_assume_role' (Grafana Assume Role — Grafana Cloud only; visible only when the awsDatasourcesTempCredentials feature toggle is on). The sixth is 'arn' (deprecated legacy value; the backend maps it to 'default').

Redshift has a 2x2 shape matrix on top of AWS auth: jsonData.useServerless picks Provisioned (false → requires jsonData.clusterIdentifier) or Serverless (true → requires jsonData.workgroupName); jsonData.useManagedSecret picks how the database credentials are obtained — false uses AWS Redshift's GetClusterCredentials (Provisioned) or GetCredentials (Serverless) to mint a temporary username/password (requires jsonData.dbUser for Provisioned; Serverless does not need it), while true reads a Secrets Manager secret (requires jsonData.managedSecret.arn — the editor also stores jsonData.managedSecret.name from the Select label; both dbUser and password come from the secret). jsonData.database is always required.

Minimal payloads per (auth × shape × credential) combination. Provisioned + temporary creds + Access-and-secret-key AWS auth: {"jsonData":{"authType":"keys","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","useServerless":false,"useManagedSecret":false,"clusterIdentifier":"my-cluster","database":"dev","dbUser":"awsuser"},"secureJsonData":{"accessKey":"<AKID>","secretKey":"<SECRET>"}}. Serverless + temporary creds + Workspace IAM Role: {"jsonData":{"authType":"ec2_iam_role","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","useServerless":true,"useManagedSecret":false,"workgroupName":"default","database":"dev"}}. Provisioned + Secrets Manager + AWS SDK Default: {"jsonData":{"authType":"default","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","useServerless":false,"useManagedSecret":true,"clusterIdentifier":"my-cluster","database":"dev","managedSecret":{"arn":"arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:123456789012:secret:redshift-1-xxxxxx","name":"redshift-1"}}}. Serverless + Secrets Manager: same as above but useServerless=true and workgroupName instead of clusterIdentifier.

Legacy auth interpretation: a provisioned config with jsonData.authType == 'arn' is treated as 'default' by the backend (awsds.AuthType.UnmarshalJSON, awsds/settings.go:87-88); a config with authType == 'sharedCreds' is normalised to 'credentials' by the backend but is NOT in this schema's allowedValues, so the schema will reject it even though the backend still loads it. Backend cluster/workgroup selection: when useManagedSecret == true the editor performs a resources/secret call and rewrites jsonData.clusterIdentifier (or, when useServerless, jsonData.workgroupName) and jsonData.dbUser from the fetched secret; the resulting stored config still needs the identifier fields populated for the backend to build the Redshift Data API request.

secureJsonData values (accessKey, secretKey, sessionToken) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.accessKey == true) instead of secureJsonData. Session token is backend-only — no editor UI writes it, but pkg/redshift/models/settings.go:67 still reads it from decrypted secure data. Pair it with authType == 'keys' for temporary AWS STS credentials via provisioning: {"secureJsonData":{"accessKey":"<AKID>","secretKey":"<SECRET>","sessionToken":"<STS-TOKEN>"}}.

Connection URL is derived, not user-input. The editor sets the datasource's root url from the cluster/workgroup endpoint plus jsonData.database — for Provisioned, `${cluster.endpoint.address}:${cluster.endpoint.port}/${database}` (src/ConfigEditor/ConfigEditor.tsx:152-161); for Serverless, `${workgroup.endpoint.address}:${workgroup.endpoint.port}/${database}` (:163-172). These come from the /resources/clusters and /resources/workgroups Grafana resource endpoints (backed by the DescribeClusters / ListWorkgroups AWS APIs). The backend does not read the root url at all — it constructs the Redshift Data API client directly from jsonData plus the resolved AWS credentials — so provisioned configs can safely omit the url; Grafana's own UI just uses it as a display value.
